jokes.text: -Rasputin, the Friendly Russian. -Wasn't he the Mad Russian?
jokes.setup: Wrestling announcer introduces 'Rasputin, the Friendly Russian'
jokes.punchline: Marge corrects the historical reference - Rasputin was 'Mad'
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical knowledge joke that shows Marge's intelligence contrasting with wrestling's manufactured personas

jokes.text: History has changed wrestling, perhaps forever.
jokes.setup: Dramatic wrestling announcement about historical impact
jokes.punchline: The absurd gravitas given to fake wrestling entertainment
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of sports announcing hyperbole applied to fake wrestling

jokes.text: The challenger from Heidelberg, Professor Burnabon Brawn. With a "W."
jokes.setup: Wrestling character introduction with academic title
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous name 'Burnabon Brawn' spelled out for clarity
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd wrestling name that combines academic pretension with macho posturing, plus the pedantic spelling clarification

jokes.text: I hope you're not taking this seriously. 5-year-olds know this is as choreographed as ballet.
jokes.setup: Family watching wrestling with typical male enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical deflation of wrestling's fake drama with ballet comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character moment - practical, sensible, and unimpressed by male posturing. The ballet comparison is unexpectedly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Look at that showoff, kissing his own muscles.
jokes.setup: Wrestler presumably flexing and posing
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal description of wrestler's self-admiring behavior
jokes.explanation: Solid visual comedy description that captures wrestling's narcissistic showmanship

jokes.text: -That's my seat. -Correction, was your seat.
jokes.setup: Kid returns from bathroom to find seat taken
jokes.punchline: Bully's pedantic correction about past vs. present ownership
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - captures schoolyard cruelty with pseudo-intellectual language. The grammar correction makes the bullying both smarter and more obnoxious.

jokes.text: But I only got up to go to the can. I don't see your name on this barstool.
jokes.setup: Victim's reasonable explanation for leaving seat
jokes.punchline: Bully's literal application of 'finders keepers' logic
jokes.explanation: Classic schoolyard bully logic that everyone recognizes from childhood experience

jokes.text: If you miss it, you better be dead or in jail. If you're in jail, break out!
jokes.setup: Standard 'don't miss it' monster truck rally promotion
jokes.punchline: Escalation to encouraging actual jailbreaks for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of promotional hyperbole to genuinely absurd criminal encouragement. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: I have an announcement. As a family growth thing... we should go to the Monster Truck Rally this Saturday.
jokes.setup: Homer announces family activity with therapeutic language
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt to disguise his own monster truck desires as family therapy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness disguised as parental responsibility. The awkward therapeutic language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I have my first solo. If you miss it, start looking for a child therapist.
jokes.setup: Lisa's important recital conflicts with monster trucks
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old threatens parents with therapy referral
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - precocious, psychologically aware, and manipulative in a sophisticated way. The therapy threat is both funny and believable.

jokes.text: -But Truck-a-saurus is one night only! -Cruel fate, why do you mock me?
jokes.setup: Homer faces choice between family obligation and monster trucks
jokes.punchline: Homer's theatrical curse at the universe over trivial entertainment conflict
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic overreaction to minor scheduling conflict. The Shakespearean language applied to monster trucks is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: -We can go to both. -Marge, you're a genius!
jokes.setup: Homer's theatrical despair over impossible choice
jokes.punchline: Homer's excessive praise for Marge's obvious scheduling solution
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike wonder at basic time management shows his simple-mindedness and appreciation for Marge

jokes.text: Series!
jokes.setup: Principal announces 'first in a series of concerts'
jokes.punchline: Homer's horrified reaction to multiple future obligations
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word captures Homer's dread at ongoing cultural obligations

jokes.text: Good, unfinished. This shouldn't take long.
jokes.setup: Announcement of Schubert's 'Unfinished Symphony'
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation that unfinished means shorter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - completely misunderstanding classical music terminology in a way that reveals his cultural ignorance but follows perfect literal reasoning

jokes.text: How much longer was Sherbert planning on making this?
jokes.setup: Homer suffering through classical concert
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'Schubert' as 'Sherbert' (ice cream)
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mispronunciation that shows his cultural ignorance while accidentally making the composer sound like dessert

jokes.text: Lisa was great. Her fingering's come a long way.
jokes.setup: Ned complimenting Lisa's piano performance
jokes.punchline: Innocent comment about piano technique that sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that works because it's Ned being completely innocent about piano technique

jokes.text: -Thanks, Flanders. Big know-it-all.
jokes.setup: Ned gives genuine compliment about Lisa's progress
jokes.punchline: Homer's resentful response to neighbor's musical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer jealousy - resenting Ned for actually knowing about music and being supportive of Lisa

jokes.text: -My son, my son. -Come on, Flanders. He's not that bad.
jokes.setup: Todd Flanders presumably plays badly
jokes.punchline: Homer's backhanded comfort to devastated father Ned
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - Homer being uncharacteristically kind while still being tactless about Todd's performance

jokes.text: I reached him. Drive defensively. The best defense is a good offense. Faster, Dad. Truck-a-saurus awaits!
jokes.setup: Family rushing to monster truck rally
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately contradicting his own driving advice while Bart adds urgency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's contradictory logic and Bart's single-minded enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Damn, no spots!
jokes.setup: Homer looking for parking at monster truck rally
jokes.punchline: Sets up the coming destruction of his car
jokes.explanation: Setup line that gains humor in retrospect when car gets destroyed

jokes.text: Pull! Pull, you dogs!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably trying to extract car from monster truck damage
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic commands to his family during car extraction
jokes.explanation: Homer treating family like sled dogs during crisis shows his dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: Let's see, cracked windshield, melted bumpers... punctured radiator, teeth marks in the trunk.
jokes.setup: Assessing damage to Homer's car from Truck-a-saurus
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd damage culminating in 'teeth marks'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of car damage from normal crash damage to monster literally biting the car. 'Teeth marks' is the perfect absurd punchline.

jokes.text: Truck-a-saurus feels very badly about what happened.
jokes.setup: Apologizing for mechanical monster destroying car
jokes.punchline: Attributing human emotions to a monster truck robot
jokes.explanation: Absurd anthropomorphization of mechanical destruction machine having feelings about property damage

jokes.text: Wow, a woman mud pool driver. Another barrier broken. Right on, sister!
jokes.setup: Female monster truck driver introduced
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old Lisa's earnest feminist celebration of monster truck gender equality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - applying her progressive politics to monster truck entertainment. The juxtaposition of feminism and mud wrestling is brilliant.

jokes.text: -Give me some nachos, Homer-saurus. -Here, Bart-a-saurus.
jokes.setup: Family enjoying monster truck atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Father and son adding '-saurus' to their names in monster truck spirit
jokes.explanation: Sweet father-son bonding through silly monster truck themed wordplay

jokes.text: He's no stranger to danger. If he's not in action, he's in traction. Captain Lance Murdock!
jokes.setup: Building up daredevil introduction with dramatic language
jokes.punchline: Action/traction rhyme that summarizes daredevil career perfectly
jokes.explanation: Excellent rhyming summary of daredevil lifestyle - the wordplay perfectly captures the boom/bust cycle of professional danger

jokes.text: I'm glad you're here to witness what may be my grisly death.
jokes.setup: Daredevil addressing audience before stunt
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully welcoming crowd to potential fatal accident
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the casual, polite way he welcomes the audience to watch him potentially die

jokes.text: ...and most frightening, the king of the jungle... one ferocious lion!
jokes.setup: Building up dangerous animals in the tank - sharks, eels, piranhas, alligators
jokes.punchline: Single lion presented as more frightening than multiple deadly water animals
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation joke about what's most dangerous in an impossible animal tank mix

jokes.text: one drop of human blood.
jokes.setup: Already insane tank of deadly animals
jokes.punchline: Adding one drop of blood as if that makes it significantly more dangerous
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd escalation - as if one drop of blood matters when there's already a lion and sharks in the same tank

jokes.text: On the chance I don't live, let me say... seat belts save lives, so buckle up!
jokes.setup: Daredevil about to attempt potentially fatal stunt
jokes.punchline: Final words are a safety message about seat belts while doing unsafe stunt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic juxtaposition - promoting safety while performing the most dangerous possible stunt. The earnest delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: On the chance I don't live, let me say... seat belts save lives, so buckle up!
jokes.setup: Daredevil about to attempt potentially fatal stunt
jokes.punchline: Giving safety advice while doing the most unsafe thing possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic juxtaposition. The contradiction between message and messenger is perfectly absurd and became highly quotable.

jokes.text: Bitchen!
jokes.setup: Daredevil successfully completes death-defying stunt
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic 1990s slang reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart reaction using era-appropriate slang showing his excitement

jokes.text: -Dad, I wanna be a daredevil. -Kids say such stupid things.
jokes.setup: Bart inspired by Lance Murdock's performance
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive parental response
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting - dismissive rather than actually addressing the concern

jokes.text: You're crazy. You'll never make it. Spare me your lectures, ladies.
jokes.setup: Kids expressing doubt about Bart's daredevil plans
jokes.punchline: Bart treating reasonable concerns as annoying lectures, calling them 'ladies'
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment - dismissing safety concerns with sexist insult shows his immaturity and bravado.

jokes.text: -My little boy! -Come on, Marge, we're mad at him.
jokes.setup: Bart injured from stunt attempt, Marge concerned
jokes.punchline: Homer reminding Marge they should maintain disciplinary stance despite injury
jokes.explanation: Great parenting dynamic - Homer's rigid adherence to punishment despite natural parental concern

jokes.text: -Are you all right? -Better than all right. I got stitches! Probably a scar too.
jokes.setup: Mother's concerned question about son's injury
jokes.punchline: Bart's excitement about medical damage as badges of honor
jokes.explanation: Perfect boy psychology - treating injury as achievement rather than consequence

jokes.text: It's tragic, but it's a small price to pay for top-notch entertainment.
jokes.setup: Doctor showing horrible injuries from TV-inspired stunts
jokes.punchline: Homer's callous cost-benefit analysis of child injury vs entertainment quality
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny Homer moment - his entertainment priorities outweigh child safety concerns. Perfectly selfish and horrible.

jokes.text: Amen!
jokes.setup: Homer's callous comment about child injury being worth entertainment
jokes.punchline: Barney's enthusiastic religious agreement with horrible sentiment
jokes.explanation: Barney supporting Homer's awful priorities with religious enthusiasm

jokes.text: Gnarly! I bet you could toss a body in there and no one would ever find it.
jokes.setup: Kids viewing Springfield Gorge on field trip
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual observation about perfect body disposal location
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Bart humor - casual criminal observation delivered as innocent sightseeing comment

jokes.text: As the only adult here, I feel I should say something. What? Cool!
jokes.setup: Otto setting up responsible adult intervention
jokes.punchline: Complete abandonment of authority for childish enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke. Otto sets up expectation of adult responsibility then completely subverts it. Perfect character moment and great economy.

jokes.text: There's a good chance I'll fall to my death. Hope to see you there!
jokes.setup: Bart announcing his gorge jump over school bus intercom
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully inviting people to watch his potential death like entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating potential death as entertainment event with polite invitation language. Perfectly captures daredevil mindset.

jokes.text: I can't explain it, but I get the same thrill from jumping stuff... that you do from reading.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to understand Bart's daredevil compulsion
jokes.punchline: Bart comparing physical danger to Lisa's intellectual pleasure
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Bart understanding that his physical thrills match Lisa's intellectual ones. Shows surprising self-awareness.

jokes.text: I can't explain it, but I get the same thrill from jumping stuff... that you do from reading.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to understand Bart's dangerous behavior
jokes.punchline: Bart comparing physical risk to Lisa's intellectual pursuits
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight showing how siblings can be complete opposites. Bart's comparison shows surprising self-awareness.

jokes.text: Doc, I heard a snap. I'm afraid it's broken. That's all of them.
jokes.setup: Lance Murdock in hospital bed with injury
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's broken every bone in his body
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of daredevil career consequences - he's literally broken everything possible

jokes.text: I've broken that dozens of times.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying another bone is broken
jokes.punchline: Lance treating repeated bone breaks as routine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lance's casual relationship with injury. Professional hazard treated as routine.

jokes.text: But the fact is: bones heal, chicks dig scars... and the U.S.A. has the best doctor to daredevil ratio in the world.
jokes.setup: Lance giving advice about daredevil career to Bart
jokes.punchline: Absurd positive spin on career that destroys your body, ending with made-up statistic
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant character advice - three increasingly absurd justifications for self-destruction, ending with completely made-up patriotic statistic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: He's got us. There's nothing we can do. He's as good as dead.
jokes.setup: Bart threatens to jump if parents don't watch him constantly
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately gives up and accepts son's death as inevitable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - immediately catastrophizing and giving up rather than actually trying to parent

jokes.text: If you don't, I'll never believe you ever again.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get Bart to promise not to jump gorge
jokes.punchline: Homer's surprisingly sophisticated understanding of promise-keeping and trust
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer showing actual parenting wisdom about trust and consequences

jokes.text: If you don't, I'll never believe you ever again. Come on. I mean it, boy!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get Bart to promise not to jump
jokes.punchline: Homer using 'never believe you again' as ultimate threat to a chronic liar
jokes.explanation: Good insight into parent-child dynamic. Homer's threat is actually meaningless given Bart's track record, but it works.

jokes.text: I should have-- And I was gonna play pickle.
jokes.setup: Homer angry at Bart for breaking promise and going to gorge anyway
jokes.punchline: Homer's anger deflating into mundane disappointment about missed game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his parental disappointment immediately shifts to personal disappointment about recreational plans

jokes.text: You'll see what it's like to watch a family member risk his life... for no good reason!
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to jump gorge himself to teach Bart lesson
jokes.punchline: Homer's plan to demonstrate parental concern through equally dangerous stunt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer parenting logic - teaching safety lesson by doing the exact same dangerous thing. Perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: I'm gonna make it! This is the greatest thrill of my life! I'm king of the world!
jokes.setup: Homer attempting gorge jump to teach Bart lesson
jokes.punchline: Homer getting caught up in daredevil excitement mid-jump
jokes.explanation: Homer completely forgetting his teaching purpose and getting caught up in the thrill - perfectly selfish character moment

jokes.text: Think you got guts? Try raising my kids!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashed at bottom of gorge, addressing crowd
jokes.punchline: Homer's parenting complaint as final statement after failed stunt
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode ending - Homer turning his parenting frustrations into daredevil bravado while lying injured. Great callback to the whole parenting struggle.

jokes.text: Think you got guts? Try raising my kids!
jokes.setup: Homer after surviving gorge fall
jokes.punchline: Claiming parenting is more dangerous than nearly dying
jokes.explanation: Perfect closing line that ties together the episode themes. Homer's perspective on parenting vs. physical danger is both funny and relatable.
